scenario.context: Station 07 of the day run: document user interfaces.
scenario.mission: You create documentation that is suitable for users and support, including versioning and change history.

customerRequest.subject: Station 07: UI documentation
customerRequest.body: Please create robust UI documentation for users and support.

questions.title: Consider the documentation target group
questions.prompt: Which contents do users and support absolutely need? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Prerequisites and limits of the function

feedback.correct: Correct: The documentation is aligned with the target audience.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please consider operating flows, error references, and versioning more strongly.

questions.title: Secure versioning
questions.prompt: What is most important for documentation versioning?

options.text: Record version, date, and reason for the change clearly
Delete old entries with every update

feedback.correct: Correct: That is the necessary basis of versioning.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Versioning needs a clear history.

questions.title: Documentation process
questions.prompt: Put the flow for clean UI documentation in order.
items: Clarify the target audience and documentation scope
Add the error and support section
correctOrder: Clarify the target audience and documentation scope
Add the error and support section

feedback.correct: Correct: The documentation process is traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please keep scope, content, and versioning in sequence.

questions.title: Documentation effort
questions.prompt: Five chapters take 24 minutes each, plus 18 minutes of review. How many minutes in total?

feedback.wrong: Please add the chapter times and review correctly.

questions.title: Documentation release note
questions.prompt: Write a documentation release note in 4-6 sentences with the target audience, version status, and an open point.
questions.expectedOutline: Release note with target-audience reference, version, and a transparent remaining task.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is directly usable for operations and support.
feedback.wrong: Please formulate the target audience, version status, and open point more clearly.
